evidence: 3 numeric columns whose headers are variant labels ('19 Zoll', 'HSK-63F mit Encoder', 'Multistep-Modul') alongside 2 identity column(s) → variants live in the header row
sheet_shape.normalization: Pivot each variant column into a (group, option, price) triple — header_to_option_name + cell_to_option_price. One normalized row per non-empty cell. The group is NOT in the source: it is supplied by the reviewer on each column's `derived.group_name`.

columns.evidence: Header matches the Artikel/Produkt keyword set; dense string values, unique per row.

columns.note: Rattle has NO Product.sku field — ProductCreateRequest exposes only name/description/base_price/currency/is_active/language/catalog_meta/integration_metadata (extra='forbid'). The customer article number is carried in integration_metadata.

samples: Konfigurierbarer Industrie-Widget für Serienfertigung mit modularem Aufbau.
Kompaktversion des Widget Pro für kleinere Losgrößen und Werkstattbetrieb.
columns.evidence: Exact keyword hit; mean sample length > 40 chars.
Exact keyword hit; numeric, dense, no zero values — a base price, not a surcharge.
Exact keyword hit; all values in the ISO currency set.
columns.note: NOT WRITABLE. ProductCreateRequest.currency is 'Accepted but ignored — currency is derived from the company's base price list.' Used here only to select the decimal_de parser.

columns.evidence: The header is a VALUE, not a field name ('19 Zoll'), and the cells are numeric → wide-variant-matrix surcharge column. The option name is the header; the GROUP appears nowhere in the sheet.
columns.note: derived.group_name 'Räder' and is_multi=false were supplied by the reviewer, NOT read from the source. Confirm both.

columns.evidence: Variant-label header ('… mit Encoder') over numeric cells → surcharge column in a wide matrix.
columns.note: derived.group_name 'Frässpindel' supplied by the reviewer. Confirm.

columns.evidence: Variant-label header over numeric cells, and one row is priced 0,00 → the module is included for Widget Pro S. A zero in a surcharge column means 'no surcharge', i.e. the option ships as standard for that product.
columns.note: Software capability. The zero-priced row is a genuine standard signal for Widget Pro S, but the baseline (no-module) state for Widget Pro still has no option — see blocker addon-only-software-modules.

columns.evidence: Best candidate 'description' scored 0.50, below the 0.60 review floor. Reviewer classified the column as internal margin/lifecycle notes — not customer-facing.
columns.note: Explicitly ignored after review, not dropped silently.

blockers.message: Column '19 Zoll' is a surcharge column with no standard sibling. The wheel variant that ships as standard is not represented anywhere in the source — no column, no zero-priced cell, no row. Without an explicit standard option, no BOM item can carry a usage_subclause for the standard wheel, and the configurator cannot remove the implicit baseline.
blockers.evidence: header '19 Zoll'; every cell > 0; no zero-priced wheel column exists in the sheet
blockers.question: Which wheel size ships as standard on Widget Pro and Widget Pro S, at what option price (expected 0), and which part does it pull into the BOM?

blockers.message: Column 'HSK-63F mit Encoder' is listed only as a surcharge. The default spindle it upgrades from is never named. Create a Frässpindel group with the default AND the upgrade as explicit options — but the default's name is not in this file.
blockers.evidence: header 'HSK-63F mit Encoder'; every cell > 0; no zero-priced spindle column exists
blockers.question: What is the default spindle on Widget Pro (e.g. ISO 30), and is 'HSK-63F ohne Encoder' also orderable? The 2026 pricelist lists only the encoder variant.

blockers.message: Column 'Multistep-Modul' prices a software module as a surcharge for Widget Pro (500,00) and as included for Widget Pro S (0,00). The baseline state for Widget Pro — the configuration WITHOUT the module — has no option representing it.
blockers.evidence: header 'Multistep-Modul'; cells 500,00 (Widget Pro) and 0,00 (Widget Pro S)
blockers.placeholder: PLACEHOLDER-STANDARD-OPTION: Software — Cyclic testing / <baseline capability unconfirmed>
blockers.question: What does Widget Pro do for cyclic testing WITHOUT the Multistep module — is there a manual/included baseline capability that should be its own price-0 option?

warnings.message: Column '19 Zoll' mapped to option_price at 0.58, below the 0.60 review floor. The role is credible (variant-label header over numeric cells) but the group is invented by the reviewer, not read from the source.
Column 'HSK-63F mit Encoder' mapped to option_price at 0.58, below the 0.60 review floor. Group supplied by the reviewer.
Column 'Bemerkung intern' best candidate 'description' scored 0.50. Classified as ignore after review — confirm with the tenant that no customer-facing text is being discarded.
Column 'Währung' (EUR) is not writable: ProductCreateRequest.currency is accepted but ignored — currency is derived from the company's base price list. Used only to select the decimal_de parser.
German decimal convention assumed (comma decimal, dot thousands) from the EUR currency column and the '12.000,00' value shape. All price columns parsed with decimal_de.
2 worksheets present (Preisliste, Notizen); profiled 'Preisliste'. Ingest one sheet per source-mapping — 'Notizen' has not been examined.

3 PLACEHOLDER-STANDARD-OPTION rows emitted into normalized-rows.json with option_price=null. They are holes with labels, not values. rattle-suggest-config MUST NOT run until the 3 blockers are answered.

notes: Sheet is a wide-variant matrix: the variants have been hoisted into the header row and the cells hold their surcharges. This shape generates implicit-base-config by construction — every variant column is a surcharge relative to a standard that appears nowhere in the file.

group_name and is_multi on columns[5..7].derived were supplied by the reviewer. Nothing in the source states that '19 Zoll' belongs to a feature called Räder, nor whether the variants are mutually exclusive.
No BOM data in this source. The parts behind each option (17-inch wheel assy, ISO 30 spindle, …) must come from a separate Stückliste — see skills/rattle-bom-builder.
Gate: blockers[] is non-empty. Answer the 3 questions with the customer, then re-ingest before running rattle-suggest-config.
